mission: Operate a premium long-haul travel company that converts demand through differentiated retailing, protects trust through superior recovery, and grows lifetime value across flights, holidays, loyalty, partner journeys, and cargo.

businessModel: Long-haul passenger revenue across premium and economy cabins
Ancillary revenue from seats, bags, upgrades, lounges, and add-ons
Holiday package margin and attach revenue
Cargo revenue on constrained long-haul capacity
Corporate and managed-travel revenue through direct and indirect channels
marketForces: Premium long-haul airlines compete on brand, schedule utility, service quality, and recovery trust rather than fare alone.
Direct digital retailing matters more as airlines seek better offer control, richer customer data, and lower indirect distribution leakage.
Alliance and joint-venture connectivity extend reach but increase complexity around servicing, disruption, and recognised customer experience.
Holiday packaging and loyalty are strategic growth levers because they deepen direct relationships and improve repeat demand.
Operational instability and airport disruption can destroy premium brand value faster than almost any other failure mode.
Cargo remains strategically important because it monetizes the same long-haul network with different demand and service economics.
keyPartners: Airports, ground handlers, and baggage partners
Corporate travel agencies and travel management companies

products.name: Cargo Digital Booking and Visibility Platform

designPrinciples: Separate guest retail, leisure packages, commercial distribution, operations recovery, and cargo because they have meaningfully different buyers, workflows, and economics.
Keep disruption recovery and customer care close to operations rather than treating them as an isolated service silo.
Centralize reusable identity, integration, payments, data, and reliability foundations because every value stream depends on them.
Treat loyalty as a growth and recognition system that spans retail, servicing, and partner journeys.
Use one shared event and data backbone so airport, digital, care, and commercial teams operate from the same facts.

recommendedStructure.mission: Own direct retail conversion, premium merchandising, loyalty engagement, and trip companion experiences.
Own package retailing, leisure servicing, and family travel economics.
Own corporate, agency, NDC, alliance, and partner commercial flows.
Own operations control tooling, disruption servicing, airport recovery, and guest fallout management.
Own cargo digital booking, visibility, and exception handling.
Provide the identity, payments, integration, data, and resilience foundations used across the airline portfolio.
Guide cross-domain strategy, architecture, design quality, and operational transformation.

operatingModelNotes: The hardest product problem is not only retail conversion; it is keeping commercial, operational, and servicing decisions connected when disruption hits.
Holiday and cargo lines should share foundations with the airline but retain product ownership because their workflows and margins differ from core passenger retail.
A 500-person organization can support this breadth only if identity, payments, data, and eventing are reused rather than rebuilt per line of business.
